title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Stock markets end flat as West Asia tensions, higher oil prices weigh on sentiment - The HinduBusinessLine
text:
Candle stick graph chart of stock market investment trading, Stock exchange concept design and background. Vector illustrations. | Photo Credit: EMOJOEZ

Sensex Today, Nifty 50 | Stock Market Highlights: After falling sharply during morning trade, the 30-share BSE Sensex rebounded by 219.9 points during the day but failed to build on the gains. The benchmark went up by 47.01 points, or 0.06%, to settle at 77,616.40. During the morning trade, it tanked 711.96 points, or 0.91%, to 76,857.43.

The 50-share NSE Nifty eked out an uptick of 4.10 points, or 0.02%, to end at 24,211, registering its third day of gains.

From the Sensex pack, Tata Consultancy Services jumped 5.43% to emerge as the top gainer, while HCL Tech climbed 5.02%, Tech Mahindra jumped 3.34%, Infosys rose by 3.17%, NTPC by 2.18% and Kotak Mahindra Bank by 1.83%.

Tata Steel, Eternal, InterGlobe Aviation, Maruti, UltraTech Cement and Bharat Electronics were among the laggards.

Brent crude, the global oil benchmark, jumped 2.57% to $77.96 per barrel.

Resilience in IT and consumer durables stocks, however, helped offset the impact of geopolitical concerns, enabling the benchmark indices to recover from early losses and close on a largely flat note, an expert said.

BSE Sensex rebounded by 219.9 points during the day but failed to build on the gains. The benchmark went up by 47.01 points, or 0.06%, to settle at 77,616.40.

Brent crude, the global oil benchmark, jumped 2.57% to $77.96 per barrel.

Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) bought equities worth ₹2,603.72 crore on Friday, according to exchange data.

The rupee depreciated 27 paise to close at 95.65 (provisional) against the US dollar on Monday, weighed down by elevated crude oil prices after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed.

With the continuous rise in prices of precious metals along with an increase in prices of some food items, retail inflation based on the Consumer Price Index (CPI) rose to 4.4 per cent in June as against 3.9 per cent of May, government reported on Monday. This is the first time since January 2025, when the headline number has crossed the 4 per cent mark.

https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/economy/retail-inflation-rose-to-44-in-june/article71216962.ece

Laser Power and Infra’s ₹742-crore initial public offering (IPO) was subscribed 38.49 times as of 4 pm on the final day of bidding, driven by robust demand from qualified institutional buyers (QIBs) and non-institutional investors (NIIs).

Strong QIB demand at 92.25 times drives overall subscription amid anchor investment of ₹223 crore

Kalpataru Limited on Monday reported a 17 per cent year-on-year rise in collections to ₹1,365 crore for the first quarter of FY 2026-27, while pre-sales grew a more modest 6 per cent to ₹1,329 crore compared to ₹1,249 crore in the same period last year. The figures are provisional and subject to limited review.

https://www.thehindubusinessline.com/markets/stock-markets/kalpataru-posts-17-jump-in-collections-launches-luxury-mumbai-project-in-q1-fy27/article71216879.ece#google_vignette

Rupee slipped to its weakest level in more than a month on Monday, as oil prices rose after ‌U.S. and Iran traded strikes, with Tehran saying it had closed the vital Strait of Hormuz.

Rupee slipped to its weakest level in more ​than a month on Monday, as oil prices rose after ‌U.S. and Iran traded strikes, with Tehran saying ​it had closed the vital Strait of Hormuz.

Bajaj Asset Management Ltd launches Bajaj Finserv BSE Top 10 Banks ETF, an open-ended ETF tracking the BSE Top 10 Banks Total Return Index. The NFO will be open from July 13–15, 2026. The fund provides cost-efficient exposure to India’s top 10 banks, offers liquidity through exchange trading, requires a minimum investment of Five Hundred rupees and carries a very high-risk classification.

Infomerics Ratings has upgraded the long-term rating of RMIL to IVR AA/Stable from IVR A and the short-term rating to IVR A1+ from IVR A1, while removing the ratings from ‘Rating Watch with Positive Implications’.

The rating action follows the successful acquisition of RMIL by Gravita India Limited (GIL), which now holds a 99.44% stake in the company. The upgrade reflects the strong operational, financial, and managerial support expected from GIL, backed by its established position in the recycling industry, diversified operations, and strong credit profile.

After a sharply lower opening, Sensex edged up 47.01 points or 0.06% to close at 77,616.40 (after hitting an intraday low of 76,857.43). Nifty 50 inched up 4.10 points or 0.02% to 24,211.

Container Corporation of India: Total volumes handled in QE June 2026 stood at 14 lakh TEUs, compared to 12 lakh TEUs in QE June 2025.

Global brokerage Goldman Sachs has turned positive on India, setting a June 2027 target of 26,500 for the Nifty, implying around 10 per cent upside from current levels, as it expects foreign institutional investor flows to return and domestic recovery to strengthen.

Goldman Sachs predicts Nifty will reach 26,500 by June 2027, driven by foreign inflows and domestic recovery.

Somany Ceramics board accorded in-principle approval for an investment of up to Rs. 58.80 crore in M/s. Siravit Ceramics Private Limited, by way of acquisition and/or subscription of equity shares and/or preference shares, in one or more tranches, resulting in the Company acquiring up to 49% of its share capital.

Shares surged 4% to Rs 538.60 on the NSE.

IndusInd Bank integrates Employees’ Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO), enabling seamless Provident Fund payments for its customers

Goldiam International board approved the allotment of 3,76,39,281 equity shares of Rs. 2/- each as fully paid-up bonus equity shares, in the proportion of 1 equity share as bonus share for every 3 existing fully paid-up equity shares of Rs. 2/- each, to the eligible members.

Borosil Ltd has approved the appointment of Mr. Ritesh Kumar, Senior Vice President, Mr. Manoj Tiwari, Vice President
